news.headline: Crude WTI +1.46% to $79.28 — 4th US Strike on Iran, Hormuz Remains Closed
news.what_happened: The US launched its 4th airstrike on Iran over the weekend targeting oil infrastructure. Iran retaliated against a Kuwaiti offshore drilling platform. Iran's IRGC reiterated the Strait of Hormuz remains closed to allied shipping.
news.why_it_matters: India imports ~500 MMT crude/year. While India has rerouted 70% of imports outside Hormuz, sustained $79+ WTI raises production costs for fuel, paints, tyres, and airlines. ONGC and OIL benefit directly via higher realization prices per barrel.

news.headline: India CPI June 4.38% — Above RBI 4% Target for First Time in 17 Months
news.what_happened: India's consumer price index for June 2026 came in at 4.38% YoY, above the RBI's 4% target and marking the first breach in nearly 1.5 years.
news.why_it_matters: Reduces probability of a near-term RBI rate cut, keeping borrowing costs elevated. Rate-sensitive sectors (NBFCs, housing finance, real estate) face a headwind as the RBI remains on hold.

news.headline: US S&P -0.79%, Nasdaq -1.55% Monday; Asian Indices Weak Tuesday Morning
news.what_happened: US equity markets fell sharply on Monday as geopolitical risk-off from the Hormuz crisis weighed on sentiment. Nikkei fell 0.93% and Hang Seng dipped 0.17% in early Asian trade on Tuesday.
news.why_it_matters: Weak global cues increase FII outflow risk from India. GIFT Nifty at 24,186 signals a mild gap-down of ~25 points for Nifty at open. Large-cap FII-heavy stocks are most exposed.

news.headline: Rupee at Rs 95.61/USD — Structural Tailwind for IT and Pharma Exporters
news.what_happened: USD/INR continues to trade at 95.61, reflecting rupee depreciation driven by elevated crude import costs and the ongoing Hormuz crisis widening India's current account deficit.
news.why_it_matters: Indian IT and pharma companies earn 60-70% of revenues in USD. At 95.61 vs a pre-crisis ~84, every dollar of billing converts to ~Rs 11.6 more — a ~13% tailwind. This will show up sharply in Q1 FY27 earnings, with Infosys reporting July 23.

news.headline: India Defense: HAL Order Book Rs 94,000 Cr; Geopolitical Catalyst Strengthens
news.what_happened: India's record Rs 6.81 lakh crore defense budget and Rs 3.3 lakh crore order pipeline are being accelerated. The ongoing US-Iran conflict has intensified India's push for indigenous defense capability.
news.why_it_matters: HAL is the primary beneficiary of Tejas fighter jet and helicopter orders. Geopolitical escalation historically triggers faster order placements and budget front-loading for India's defense PSUs.

sector_outlook.reason: WTI crude +1.46% to $79.28; ONGC realization price rises directly
Geopolitical escalation accelerates India defense procurement; HAL Rs 94,000Cr order book
Rupee at 95.61/USD = ~13% tailwind vs pre-crisis; INFY Q1 FY27 results July 23
Rupee weakness provides margin expansion; defensive positioning during risk-off
Inventory gains from crude rise, but govt pricing pressure risk if crude stays high
Hang Seng flat, China GDP resilient but no fresh stimulus — metals range-bound
India CPI 4.38% > RBI 4% target — no near-term rate cut; borrowing costs stay elevated
RBI hawkish on inflation — rate cuts deferred; home loan rates stay high
Crude +1.46% + rupee at 95.61 = dual cost squeeze on jet fuel and dollar-denominated leases
Crude-derived raw material costs rising on Hormuz tensions

picks.catalyst: WTI Crude +1.46% to $79.28/bbl; Strait of Hormuz tensions persist
picks.logic: ONGC's net realization per barrel tracks international crude prices. Every $1 rise in Brent adds ~Rs 500-700 Cr/quarter to ONGC's net revenue. With the Hormuz crisis keeping crude structurally elevated and fresh US-Iran strikes overnight, ONGC is the most direct P&L beneficiary in Indian markets.

picks.catalyst: US-Iran conflict and Strait of Hormuz closure accelerating India's indigenous defense procurement
picks.logic: HAL carries an order book of ~Rs 94,000 Cr (Tejas fighters, light combat helicopters, Dornier aircraft). Geopolitical tensions have historically triggered India's defense budget acceleration and faster order placements. With the US-Iran conflict ongoing, HAL is structurally positioned as India's strategic asset beneficiary.

picks.catalyst: USD/INR at Rs 95.61 (extreme rupee weakness) + Q1 FY27 results due July 23, 2026
picks.logic: Infosys derives ~65% of revenues in USD. At 95.61 vs pre-crisis ~84, every $1M of billing translates to ~Rs 1.16 Cr extra — a structural ~13% revenue tailwind in INR terms. Q1 FY27 earnings (July 23) will reflect this. Stock likely to re-rate ahead of results on margin expansion expectations.

risks: If crude spikes beyond $85 WTI on further Hormuz escalation, India macro deteriorates sharply — broad market sell-off likely
FII flip to net sellers would pressure large-cap watch zones — monitor FII data at 12:30 PM
RBI may signal hawkish commentary post CPI breach — additional headwind for rate-sensitives
Today is not expiry day (weekly expiry Thursday July 16) but elevated intraday volatility expected on geopolitical overhang
If GIFT Nifty gap-down widens beyond 0.5% at open, reduce position size
